_comment: Wizard step definitions for each AI tag. Loaded by the CSR wizard widget.
_source: Sadie Hardy — HOW TO DO EVERYTHING PARKM doc

needs_tag.label: Needs Tag
needs_tag.intro: The AI was unable to classify this ticket. Human review is required.

steps.text: Read the full email carefully and determine the customer's actual need
Search parkm.app by customer email for context
Classify manually: What does this customer actually need?
steps.substep: Set the 'Agent Corrected Tags' field to the correct classification so the AI can learn from this case.

steps.text: Follow the appropriate process for the correct tag type

validation_on_close: Did you set the Agent Corrected Tags field?
Did you handle the underlying request?

customer_canceling_a_permit_and_refundin.label: Cancel Permit + Refund
customer_canceling_a_permit_and_refundin.intro: Customer wants to cancel their permit AND get a refund. Refund window is 30 days from the LAST charge date.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Open the Vehicles and Permits tab
steps.substep: Confirm the license plate and permit. Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}

steps.text: Check if permit is already canceled
steps.substep: If already canceled, note the cancellation date for eligibility calculation.

steps.text: Open the Payments tab - find the last charge date and amount
steps.substep: Refund window is 30 days from the LAST charge date.

steps.text: Verify move-out date is within the 30-day refund window
steps.substep: Move-out date from email: {{move_out_date}}. If not found, ask the customer.

steps.text: Set permit to Delay Cancel (1 week out)
steps.substep: Do NOT cancel immediately. Use Actions > Cancel > Delay Cancellation and set to 1 week. Delete the 'Next Recurring Date' so they are not charged again. Tell resident: 'Your permit is set to cancel on [date]. You will not be charged again.'

steps.text: Determine refund eligibility

options.label: Eligible - Forward to Accounting
Deny - Outside 30-Day Window

steps.text: If eligible: Forward WHOLE thread to accounting@parkm.com
steps.substep:
Include: customer email on account, refund amount, reason (e.g. 'Moved Out'), and property name. Format:
[email]
$[amount]
[reason]
[property name]
Then set ticket status to 'Waiting on Accounting'.

steps.text: Send response email to customer
Update ticket status (Waiting on Accounting or Closed)

validation_on_close: Did you verify the move-out date is within 30 days of the last charge?
Did you set the permit to delay cancel (not immediate)?
Did you delete the Next Recurring Date?
Did you forward to accounting@parkm.com (if eligible) OR send denial?

customer_inquiring_for_grandfathered_per.label: Grandfathered Permit Inquiry
customer_inquiring_for_grandfathered_per.intro: Customer is asking about a grandfathered permit. Grandfathered permits are free permits issued when a complex approves it. The COMPLEX must email us to approve.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Check if the customer already has a grandfathered permit
steps.substep: Look under Vehicles and Permits for a permit labeled 'grandfathered' or a free permit.

steps.text: Does the customer have complex approval?
steps.substep: The leasing office/property manager MUST email support@parkm.com to approve a grandfathered permit. The customer cannot self-approve.

options.label: Complex has approved - Issue free permit
No approval yet - Direct customer to leasing office

steps.text: If approved: Issue a free permit
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Select community > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration to 1 year (or date from complex if provided, otherwise default 1 year and adjust later). Label permit 'Grandfathered' under custom permit number. Do NOT enter a Next Recurring Date.

steps.text: If no approval: Direct customer to their leasing office
steps.substep: Tell them their leasing office needs to email support@parkm.com to approve the grandfathered permit with their vehicle info.

steps.text: Send confirmation to customer and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify complex approval before issuing a free permit?
If issued, did you label the permit 'Grandfathered'?

customer_inquiring_for_locked_down_permi.label: Locked Down Permit Inquiry
customer_inquiring_for_locked_down_permi.intro: Customer is asking about a locked/restricted permit type they cannot purchase on their own. The property has restricted certain permits (e.g., carport, garage) and requires approval. PENDING: Confirm exact process with Sadie.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Check Zoho CRM notes for the property
steps.substep: Some properties restrict permit types (e.g., carport, garage) to specific resident classifications. Check if the property has notes about which permits require approval.

steps.text: Determine if the customer needs a resident type change or override
steps.substep: The leasing office may need to email us to approve the customer for the restricted permit type. If so, direct the customer to speak to their leasing office first.

options.label: Property has approved - Do override/change resident type
No approval - Direct to leasing office

steps.text: If approved: Change resident type or open permits as needed
steps.substep: Go to the customer's account > Edit > Change customer classification to the approved type. Then they can purchase the permit themselves, or you can issue it for them.

steps.text: Send response to customer and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify property approval before making changes?
Did you respond to the customer?

customer_inquiring_for_additional_permit.label: Additional Permit Inquiry
customer_inquiring_for_additional_permit.intro: Customer wants an additional permit added to their account. Some properties restrict extra permits and require an override/approval from the leasing office.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Check how many permits the customer currently has
steps.substep: Look at Vehicles and Permits tab. Note how many active permits exist.

steps.text: Check if the property allows additional permits
steps.substep: Check Zoho CRM notes for the property. Some properties cap the number of permits per unit. If restricted, the leasing office must email to approve.

steps.text: Is the additional permit approved?

options.label: Allowed or property approved - Help purchase/issue permit
Needs property approval - Direct to leasing office

steps.text: If approved: Open permits/do override if needed, then help customer purchase
steps.substep: If locked down, you may need to open up permits or change resident type first. Then either help them buy online or purchase on backend via Actions > Sale Permit.

steps.text: Send response to customer and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify the property allows additional permits?
Did you respond to the customer?

customer_rental_car.label: Rental Car
customer_rental_car.intro: Customer has a rental car (their car is in the shop, etc.) and needs a temporary permit. Issue a FREE temporary permit for the rental - do NOT modify their existing permit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Confirm the customer has an existing active permit on their regular vehicle
steps.substep: Their regular permit should stay in place. We do NOT want them to modify it because they often forget to switch back.

steps.text: Ask how long they expect to have the rental
steps.substep: If they say 2 weeks, issue for 4 weeks to allow buffer time. Never set it to 2055.

steps.text: Issue a FREE temporary permit for the rental vehicle
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Select community > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration (double the expected rental time). Label it 'Temp permit' under custom permit number. Add rental vehicle info (plate, make, model, etc.). Do NOT enter a Next Recurring Date.

steps.text: Send confirmation to customer with screenshot of the permit preview
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue a FREE temporary permit (not modify existing)?
Did you set a reasonable expiration date (not 2055)?
Did you confirm with the customer?

customer_double_charged_or_extra_charges.label: Double Charged / Extra Charges
customer_double_charged_or_extra_charges.intro: Customer is reporting a billing issue - double charge, unexpected charge, or extra fees. Investigate in the Payments tab before responding.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Open the Payments & Transactions tab
steps.substep: Look for duplicate charges, unexpected amounts, failed payment retries, convenience fees, or credit card surcharges.

steps.text: Confirm the license plate matches their account
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}

steps.text: Determine the cause of the charge
steps.substep:
Common causes:
- Convenience fee / credit card surcharge (these are normal and disclosed)
- Permit renewed as expected (customer forgot)
- Actual duplicate charge (system error)
- NSF fee (non-sufficient funds)
- Taxes on the permit

options.label: Legitimate charge - Explain to customer
Duplicate/error - Forward to accounting for refund

steps.text: If duplicate/error: Forward WHOLE thread to accounting@parkm.com
steps.substep:
Include: email on account, refund amount, reason, property name.
Then set ticket to 'Waiting on Accounting'.

steps.text: If legitimate: Explain the charge clearly to the customer
steps.substep: Be helpful and clear. Explain convenience fees, surcharges, or recurring permit charges as applicable.

steps.text: Send response and update ticket status

validation_on_close: Did you investigate the charge(s) in the Payments tab?
Did you either explain the charge OR forward to accounting?

customer_guest_permit_and_pricing_questi.label: Guest Permit & Pricing
customer_guest_permit_and_pricing_questi.intro: Customer is asking about guest permits, pricing, or how guest permits work.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email (if they have an account)
Check the property's guest permit setup in parkm.app
steps.substep: Go to the property > check guest permit types, pricing, duration limits, and any restrictions. Some properties have limits on guest permits.

steps.text: Answer the customer's question about guest permits
steps.substep:
Key info:
- Guest permits are purchased by the RESIDENT for their guest's vehicle
- Multiple people can buy guest permits for the same vehicle
- Guest permits may have limits per property
- Guests can pre-purchase permits
- Residents can extend guest permits
- Direct them to: https://parkm.app/permit/community?forceChange=true

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you answer the customer's specific question about guest permits/pricing?

customer_miscellaneous_questions.label: Miscellaneous Question
customer_miscellaneous_questions.intro: General customer question that does not fit other categories. Read carefully and respond with a clear, helpful answer.

steps.text: Read the full email carefully
Search parkm.app by customer email (if account-specific)
Research the answer using Zoho CRM notes and parkm.app
steps.substep: Check property notes in Zoho CRM for any special rules. If unsure, email internalmanagers@parkm.com for guidance.

steps.text: Compose a clear, friendly, and informative response
steps.substep: Remember: the goal is to give residents clear and informative answers, even if it means writing a more detailed email.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you fully answer the customer's question?

customer_sending_money_order.label: Customer Money Order
customer_sending_money_order.intro: Customer is paying by money order. Issue a free permit for the paid duration while waiting for the money order to be processed.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Confirm vehicle info and which property/permit type they need
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}

steps.text: Issue a FREE permit for the duration they are paying for
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration to match the period they paid for (e.g., 1 month). Label it with the money order reference if available.

steps.text: Confirm the money order payment details
steps.substep: Note: money orders are handled separately from online payments. The money order will be processed by accounting.

steps.text: Send confirmation to customer and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue a free permit for the paid duration?
Did you confirm with the customer?

customer_need_help_buying_a_permit.label: Help Buying a Permit
customer_need_help_buying_a_permit.intro: Customer needs help purchasing a permit for the first time.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email - check if they have an account
If no account: Direct them to create one
steps.substep:
Website: https://parkm.app/permit/community?forceChange=true
OR: https://www.parkm.com/

steps.text: Identify the issue preventing purchase
steps.substep:
Common issues:
- Can't find their community
- Resident type is wrong (needs to be changed)
- Permits are locked down (needs override)
- Payment method issues
- Error message during checkout

steps.text: If there's a system error preventing purchase: Issue a free temporary permit
steps.substep: Issue a free permit for a few days/1 week while Internal Managers investigate the error. Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with details of the error.

steps.text: If you can resolve it: Help them purchase or guide them through the process
steps.substep: You can purchase on the backend: Actions > Sale Permit. Make sure they have a payment method on file and you have permission to charge it. Toggle OFF 'Issue Free Permit' and select their payment method.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you help the customer purchase or explain how to?
If there was an error, did you issue a temp permit and escalate?

customer_need_help_renewing_permit.label: Help Renewing Permit
customer_need_help_renewing_permit.intro: Customer needs help renewing their existing permit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Check the permit status and next recurring date
steps.substep: Is it a recurring paid permit? Check the next recurring date and expiration date. Recurring permits auto-renew by pushing out the expiration date each charge cycle.

steps.text: Identify the renewal issue
steps.substep:
Common issues:
- Payment method expired/declined
- Permit was accidentally canceled
- Need to change charge date
- Permit expired and needs reactivation

options.label: Payment issue - Update payment method
Permit expired - Reactivate
Need to change charge date

steps.text: Resolve the issue
steps.substep:
For payment: Have customer update card at https://parkm.app/account/login
For reactivation: May need to issue a new permit
For charge date: Actions > Move Next Recurring Date

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify the permit status and resolve the renewal issue?
Did you respond to the customer?

customer_need_help_creating_an_account.label: Help Creating Account
customer_need_help_creating_an_account.intro: Customer is having trouble creating a ParkM account.

steps.text: Check if an account already exists for this email
steps.substep: Search parkm.app by email. The customer may already have an account and not realize it.

steps.text: If account exists: Send them a password reset
steps.substep: Click 'Reset Password' button on their account, or direct them to https://parkm.app/account/login > 'Forgot Password'

steps.text: If no account: Guide them to create one
steps.substep:
Direct them to: https://parkm.app/permit/community?forceChange=true
They will need to search for their community and create an account.

steps.text: If there's a technical error: Issue a free temp permit and escalate
steps.substep: Issue a free permit for a few days/1 week. Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with the error details so they can investigate.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you help the customer create an account or resolve their issue?

customer_new_towing_legislation_no_parki.label: Towing Legislation / No Parking
customer_new_towing_legislation_no_parki.intro: Customer is asking about towing laws or no-parking rules. PENDING: Confirm exact process with Sadie.

steps.text: Read the email carefully to understand the specific question
Remind the customer that ParkM is NOT a towing company
steps.substep: ParkM sells parking permits. We do not tow, boot, or ticket vehicles. For towing questions, they should contact the towing company or their leasing office.

steps.text: If unsure how to respond, escalate to Internal Managers
steps.substep: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with the thread and a summary.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you respond to the customer?

customer_no_plate_or_expired_tags.label: No Plate / Expired Tags
customer_no_plate_or_expired_tags.intro: Customer's vehicle has no physical plates or expired registration tags. ParkM permits are tied to license plates, so they must resolve this first.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Explain that ParkM permits are tied to license plates
steps.substep: The license plate must always be accurate on their permit to avoid towing/booting/ticketing issues.

steps.text: Direct the customer to their leasing office
steps.substep: They need to speak to their complex about the expired tags / no plates situation. In the meantime, they need to park OFF SITE since ParkM permits require valid plates.

steps.text: Send response using template
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you direct the customer to their leasing office?
Did you explain they need to park off site until resolved?

customer_someone_is_parking_in_my_spot.label: Someone in My Spot
customer_someone_is_parking_in_my_spot.intro: Customer is reporting that someone is parking in their assigned space.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Verify the customer's assigned space number
steps.substep: Check their permit for the space number. Extracted space: {{space_number}}

steps.text: Direct the customer to their leasing office
steps.substep: ParkM does not enforce parking spaces or tow vehicles. The customer should contact their leasing office about the unauthorized vehicle. The leasing office can then contact the towing company if needed.

steps.text: If we monitor the property: Forward to Brian McDonough
steps.substep: Check Zoho CRM for 'ParkM Monitoring' checkmark. If we monitor: forward to Brian McDonough so monitors can be alerted. If we don't monitor: direct customer to their monitoring/towing company.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you direct the customer to their leasing office?
Did you check if ParkM monitors the property?

customer_parking_space_not_in_dropdown.label: Space Not in Dropdown
customer_parking_space_not_in_dropdown.intro: Customer's parking space number is not listed in the system dropdown when purchasing a permit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Check the property's space list in parkm.app
steps.substep: Go to the property and check if the space exists. It may need to be added.

steps.text: If the space is missing: Escalate to Internal Managers
steps.substep: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com and ask them to add the missing space to the property. Include the space number and property name.

steps.text: Issue a free temporary permit while waiting
steps.substep: Issue a free permit for a few days so the customer is covered. Actions > Sale Permit > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON.

steps.text: Send response and close/hold ticket

validation_on_close: Did you escalate the missing space to Internal Managers?
Did you issue a temp permit to cover the customer?

quick_templates.label: Space Not Found - Working On It

customer_password_reset.label: Password Reset
customer_password_reset.intro: Customer needs help resetting their password.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
steps.substep: Verify the account exists and the email matches.

steps.text: Reset password using one of these methods
steps.substep:
Option 1: Direct them to https://parkm.app/account/login > 'Forgot Password'
Option 2: Find their account > click 'Reset Password' button
Option 3: Manual reset via Manage User > change password to 'Parking'

steps.text: If manual reset, send login credentials
steps.substep:
Format:
Username: [their email]
Password: Parking
https://parkm.app/permit/community

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you reset the password or send instructions?
Did you confirm with the customer?

customer_towed_booted_ticketed.label: Towed / Booted / Ticketed
customer_towed_booted_ticketed.intro: Customer's vehicle was towed, booted, or ticketed. Be empathetic. ParkM does NOT tow/boot/ticket - we only sell permits. Offer a proof of permit if applicable.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Check if the vehicle had an active permit at the time of the tow/boot
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}. Check the vehicle's permit history - was the permit active when the tow/boot occurred?

steps.text: Remind the customer that ParkM is NOT the towing company
steps.substep: ParkM simply sells parking permits. We do not tow, boot, or ticket vehicles. Express empathy: 'I understand how frustrating being towed/booted can be...'

steps.text: Determine if a proof of permit is applicable
steps.substep: Only provide proof of permit if the vehicle appeared to have a permit at/around the time of the incident. Proof of permit covers the last 72 hours (3 days) only. If the incident was more than 72 hours ago, they need to work with the tow company using their original receipt.

options.label: Vehicle was permitted - Generate Proof of Permit
Vehicle was NOT permitted - Direct to tow company

steps.text: If permitted: Generate Proof of Permit
steps.substep: Go to account > Actions > Proof of Permit. Either email it directly (add tow company email if known) or download the PDF and attach it to the thread. Check Zoho CRM for tow company email. After sending, STAY OUT OF IT - do not promise refunds or releases!

steps.text: If not permitted: Direct to towing company
steps.substep: Look up the tow company for the property in Zoho CRM or in .app under the property's enforcement company. Share the tow company PHONE NUMBER (not email). If tow company is unknown, advise them to look for towing signs posted at the property.

steps.text: NEVER promise a refund or release of the vehicle
steps.substep: Tow/boot refunds are EXTREMELY rare and require Internal Manager approval. If the customer insists, forward to internalmanagers@parkm.com.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you check the vehicle's permit status at the time of the incident?
Did you provide a proof of permit (if applicable)?
Did you remind the customer ParkM does not tow/boot/ticket?
Did you NOT promise a refund or vehicle release?

customer_warned_or_tagged.label: Warning / Tag
customer_warned_or_tagged.intro: Customer received a warning sticker or tag on their vehicle. Tags are warnings with no fees. Focus on helping them get properly permitted.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Ask the customer to send a picture of the tag
steps.substep: This helps identify the reason for the tag and who placed it. In Colorado, ParkM's tags are ORANGE. Other colors = not us.

steps.text: Check the vehicle's permit status
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}. Is the vehicle permitted? Was the plate correct? Check tow history/violations on the vehicle in .app.

steps.text: Determine why they were tagged
steps.substep: Common reasons: no ParkM permit, expired permit, wrong plate on permit, parked in fire lane, parked in handicap spot without placard, parked in someone's assigned spot, expired vehicle tags.

steps.text: Help them resolve the issue
steps.substep:
If not permitted: help them get a permit
If plate was wrong: update the plate
If other violation: explain and advise
Note: sticker removal is the customer's responsibility - ParkM cannot remove it.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you identify why they were tagged?
Did you help them resolve the underlying issue?
Did you clarify that tags are warnings with no fees?

customer_payment_help.label: Payment Help
customer_payment_help.intro: Customer needs general help with payments - can't pay, payment failed, need to update card, etc.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Open the Payments & Transactions tab
steps.substep: Check for failed payments, expired cards, or missing payment methods.

steps.text: Identify the payment issue
steps.substep:
Common issues:
- Credit/debit card expired or declined
- No payment method on file
- Wants to change payment method
- Bank account issues
- Convenience fee questions

options.label: Card expired/declined - Guide to update
No payment method - Help add one
Convenience fee question - Explain

steps.text: Help resolve the payment issue
steps.substep: For card updates: Direct them to https://parkm.app/account/login to update their payment method. Or you can delete the old method on backend: Payments tab > delete old card. Note: we can only delete payment methods, NOT add them on the backend.

steps.text: If payment is stuck and they need to park tonight: Issue a free temp permit
steps.substep: Issue for a few days while the payment issue is resolved.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you identify and resolve the payment issue?
Did you respond to the customer?

customer_update_vehicle_info.label: Update Vehicle Info
customer_update_vehicle_info.intro: Customer needs to update their vehicle information (license plate, make, model, etc.).

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Locate the vehicle they want to update
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}

steps.text: Determine what needs to be updated
steps.substep:
LICENSE PLATE change: Actions > Modify License Plate > Enter new plate > Toggle 'Transfer All Active Permits' > Save
OTHER changes (make, model, VIN, color): Actions > Edit Vehicle > Make changes > Save

steps.text: Make the update in parkm.app
Verify the change saved correctly
steps.substep: Refresh the page and confirm the update is reflected.

steps.text: Send confirmation to customer
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify the update saved correctly?
Did you use 'Modify License Plate' (not 'Edit Vehicle') for plate changes?
Did you transfer active permits to the new plate?

customer_update_contact_info.label: Update Contact Info
customer_update_contact_info.intro: Customer wants to update their email, phone, address, or unit number.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by customer email address
Identify what contact info needs updating
steps.substep: Common updates: email address, phone number, unit number.

steps.text: Update the info in parkm.app
steps.substep: Go to the customer's account > Manage User or Edit to update email/phone/unit.

steps.text: Verify the change saved correctly
Send confirmation to customer
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify the update saved?
Did you confirm with the customer?

property_changing_resident_type_for_appr.label: Property: Change Resident Type
property_changing_resident_type_for_appr.intro: Property manager is requesting a resident type change so a resident can purchase a specific permit type (e.g., carport, garage).

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Confirm which resident type the property is requesting
steps.substep: Check the property notes in Zoho CRM. Example: at some properties, residents need to be 'Resident X' to get carport/garage permits.

steps.text: Change the resident type
steps.substep: Go to the resident's account > Edit > Change 'Customer Classification' to the requested type > Save.

steps.text: Notify the property that the change has been made
steps.substep: Let them know the resident can now purchase the approved permit type.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you change the resident type as requested?
Did you notify the property?

property_approving_grandfathered_permit.label: Property: Approve Grandfathered Permit
property_approving_grandfathered_permit.intro: Property is approving a grandfathered (free) permit for a resident. Issue a free permit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name (from property's email)
Get the vehicle details from the property's email
steps.substep: Need: license plate, make, model, year, color, state. If missing, ask the property.

steps.text: Issue a free grandfathered permit
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Select community > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration to the date provided by property (if none given, default to 1 year and adjust later). Label permit 'Grandfathered'. Do NOT enter a Next Recurring Date.

steps.text: Confirm with the property that the permit has been issued
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue the free permit and label it 'Grandfathered'?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_approving_override_additional_p.label: Property: Approve Override/Additional Permit
property_approving_override_additional_p.intro: Property is approving an override so a resident can get an additional or restricted permit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Open up permits or change resident type as needed
steps.substep:
Depending on the property setup, you may need to:
- Change the resident's customer classification
- Do an override to allow additional permits
- Open up a specific permit type

steps.text: Help the resident purchase or issue the permit
steps.substep: Either guide the resident to purchase online, or purchase/issue on the backend via Actions > Sale Permit.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you complete the override/permit issuance?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_extending_expiration_date_on_a_.label: Property: Extend Permit Expiration
property_extending_expiration_date_on_a_.intro: Property is requesting an extension on a permit's expiration date.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Locate the permit to extend
Determine the permit type and use the correct method
steps.substep:
RECURRING PAID permits: The 'Extend Expiration Date' button does NOT work on these. Use 'Move Next Recurring Date' instead.
FREE or ONE-TIME permits: Use either 'Extend Expiration Date' or 'Delay Cancellation' - both work.

steps.text: Extend the permit
steps.substep: Actions > Extend Expiration Date (or Move Next Recurring Date for recurring). Set the new date as requested by the property.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you use the correct method based on permit type?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_audits_or_reports.label: Property: Audit / Report Request
property_audits_or_reports.intro: Property is requesting an audit, report, or parking statistics.

steps.text: Identify what type of report the property needs
steps.substep: Common requests: permit list, vehicle list, payment history, space assignments, monitoring activity.

steps.text: Check if the report can be generated from parkm.app
steps.substep: Some reports can be pulled from the admin side. Check the property's data in .app.

steps.text: If you cannot generate the report: Forward to Internal Managers
steps.substep: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with the property's request details.

steps.text: Send the report or let the property know it's being prepared
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you provide the report or escalate to Internal Managers?

property_checking_if_a_vehicle_is_permit.label: Property: Check Vehicle Permit
property_checking_if_a_vehicle_is_permit.intro: Property is asking if a specific vehicle/plate is permitted. Provide COMPLETE information - not just 'yes' or 'no'.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the license plate provided
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}

steps.text: Check permit status and gather full details
steps.substep:
DO NOT just say 'yes'. Include ALL of:
- The date the permit became active on that plate
- The specific community the permit is for
- The type of permit (carport, garage, 1st car, open lot, etc.)
- Current status (active, expired, canceled)

steps.text: Understand the context
steps.substep: Properties often ask because a vehicle was tagged, booted, towed, or ticketed. A simple 'yes' can cause confusion - they may think the tow company made a mistake when the resident actually just updated their plate recently.

steps.text: Send detailed response to the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you include the activation date, community, and permit type?
Did you NOT just say 'yes' or 'no'?

property_checking_who_has_a_space_number.label: Property: Who Has This Space?
property_checking_who_has_a_space_number.intro: Property is asking who is assigned to a specific parking space number.

steps.text: Search parkm.app for the space number at the property
steps.substep: Go to the property > check space assignments. Extracted space: {{space_number}}

steps.text: Find who has a permit with that space number
Respond with the resident's name and vehicle info for that space
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you provide the space assignment info to the property?

property_checking_who_is_in_a_unit.label: Property: Who is in This Unit?
property_checking_who_is_in_a_unit.intro: Property is asking who is registered in a specific apartment unit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by unit number at the property
steps.substep: Extracted unit: {{unit_number}}

steps.text: Find residents registered for that unit
Respond with the resident name(s) and vehicle info for the unit
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you provide the unit info to the property?

property_inquiring_about_a_tow_boot_tick.label: Property: Tow/Boot/Ticket Inquiry
property_inquiring_about_a_tow_boot_tick.intro: Property is asking about a tow, boot, or ticket at their community. ParkM does NOT tow/boot/ticket. Be helpful but stay within our means.

steps.text: Search parkm.app for the vehicle/plate in question
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}

steps.text: Check permit status and tow history
steps.substep: Click on the vehicle > scroll down to 'Tow History'. Also check the account 'Overview' tab for tow incidents.

steps.text: Provide the property with the vehicle's permit status
steps.substep: Include: permit active date, community, permit type, and any violations/tow history logged.

steps.text: If property wants a tow/boot refunded: Forward to Internal Managers
steps.substep: Tow/boot/ticket refunds are only handled by Internal Managers. Forward the thread to internalmanagers@parkm.com with a summary.

steps.text: Direct the property to their towing company for tow-specific questions
steps.substep: Check Zoho CRM or .app for the property's enforcement company. Share the tow company contact info with the property.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you provide permit status information?
If refund requested, did you forward to Internal Managers?

property_inquiring_about_a_warning_tag.label: Property: Warning/Tag Inquiry
property_inquiring_about_a_warning_tag.intro: Property is asking about a warning tag placed on a vehicle.

steps.text: Check if ParkM monitors this property
steps.substep: Check Zoho CRM for 'ParkM Monitoring' checkmark. In Colorado, ParkM monitoring uses ORANGE tags.

steps.text: Search parkm.app for the vehicle/plate in question
steps.substep: Check violations/tow history on the vehicle.

steps.text: If ParkM monitors: Explain why the vehicle was tagged
steps.substep: Check the violation log. Common reasons: no permit, expired permit, fire lane, handicap without placard, wrong spot, expired vehicle tags.

steps.text: If ParkM does NOT monitor: Direct to their monitoring company
steps.substep: Let them know ParkM did not place the tag. They should contact their monitoring/enforcement company.

steps.text: If something seems incorrect about the tag: Report to Brian McDonough and Internal Managers
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you check if ParkM monitors the property?
Did you respond to the property?

property_monitor_request.label: Property: Monitor Request
property_monitor_request.intro: Property is requesting monitoring, tagging, or patrol of their lot. PENDING: Confirm if this is for new monitoring setup or specific vehicle requests with Sadie.

steps.text: Determine the type of monitoring request
steps.substep:
Is this a request to:
- Tag/warn a specific vehicle
- Start monitoring their lot
- Report an issue with current monitoring

steps.text: If requesting a specific vehicle to be tagged/warned
steps.substep:
Check if ParkM monitors the property (Zoho CRM 'ParkM Monitoring' checkmark).
IF WE MONITOR: Tell property we'll let our monitors know, then forward thread to Brian McDonough.
IF WE DON'T MONITOR: Direct them to their monitoring/towing company.

steps.text: If requesting to set up monitoring: CC the sales rep
steps.substep: CC the property's ParkM sales rep and let them handle the monitoring setup discussion.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you check if ParkM monitors the property?
Did you forward to Brian if applicable?

property_leasing_staff_login.label: Property: Leasing Staff Login
property_leasing_staff_login.intro: Property manager or leasing office staff needs help with their login to the Property Manager Portal.

steps.text: Determine if this is a new setup or a password reset

options.label: New PM/leasing agent setup
Password reset for existing user

steps.text: If password reset: Find the user in Administration
steps.substep: Go to Administration > Users > Paste their email > Toggle 'Show Tenant Users' ON. Click Actions > Reset Password (to send reset email) or Edit (to manually set password to 'Parking').

steps.text: If new setup: Forward to Internal Managers
steps.substep: New Property Manager portal setups should be handled by Internal Managers. Forward the request to internalmanagers@parkm.com.

steps.text: Send login credentials if reset
steps.substep:
Format:
Username: [their email]
Password: Parking
Portal: [Property Manager Portal URL]

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you resolve the login issue or escalate for new setup?

property_miscellaneous_questions.label: Property: Misc Question
property_miscellaneous_questions.intro: General question from a property manager that doesn't fit other categories.

steps.text: Read the full email carefully
Check Zoho CRM for property notes and sales rep info
If unsure: Email Internal Managers for guidance
steps.substep: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with the thread and a summary. They will reply with guidance.

steps.text: Compose a clear, helpful response
steps.substep: Be extra helpful with property managers. Give thorough, informative answers.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you fully answer the property's question?

property_sending_money_order.label: Property: Money Order
property_sending_money_order.intro: Property is sending a money order payment on behalf of a resident.

steps.text: Get the resident's account info and vehicle details
Issue a free permit for the paid duration
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration to match the paid period.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue the free permit for the paid duration?

property_update_or_register_employee_veh.label: Property: Employee Vehicles
property_update_or_register_employee_veh.intro: Property wants to add or update employee vehicles. Employee vehicles get free permits for life (set expiration to 2055).

steps.text: Get the employee vehicle details from the property
steps.substep: Need: license plate, make, model, year, color, state, and employee name/email.

steps.text: Search for the employee in parkm.app or create an account if needed
Issue a free permit for life
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration to year 2055. You can also add the vehicle to the property's 'Employee Car' list.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue the free permit with 2055 expiration?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_update_resident_vehicle.label: Property: Update Resident Vehicle
property_update_resident_vehicle.intro: Property is requesting a vehicle update on behalf of a resident.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Locate the vehicle to update
Make the update
steps.substep:
LICENSE PLATE: Actions > Modify License Plate > Enter new plate > Transfer All Active Permits > Save
OTHER: Actions > Edit Vehicle > Update fields > Save

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify the update saved?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_update_resident_contact_informa.label: Property: Update Resident Contact Info
property_update_resident_contact_informa.intro: Property is requesting a contact info update for a resident (email, phone, unit).

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's current email or name
Update the contact information as requested
steps.substep: Go to the account > Manage User or Edit to update email, phone, or unit number.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you update the info and confirm with the property?

property_update_resident_password.label: Property: Reset Resident Password
property_update_resident_password.intro: Property is requesting a password reset on behalf of a resident.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email
Reset the password
steps.substep:
Option 1: Click 'Reset Password' button on the account
Option 2: Manage User > change password to 'Parking'
Option 3: Administration > Users > search email > Toggle 'Show Tenant Users' > Actions > Reset Password

steps.text: Reply to the property with the resident's login info
steps.substep:
Format:
Username: [resident email]
Password: Parking
https://parkm.app/permit/community

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you reset the password and provide login info?

property_register_resident_account_for_t.label: Property: Register Resident Account
property_register_resident_account_for_t.intro: Property wants us to create a ParkM account for a resident.

steps.text: Get the resident's info from the property
steps.substep: Need: name, email, phone, unit number, and vehicle details (plate, make, model, year, color, state).

steps.text: Check if an account already exists for this email
If no account: Create one in parkm.app
steps.substep: Create the account with the resident's info and add their vehicle.

steps.text: Confirm with the property and provide login credentials
steps.substep:
Format:
Username: [resident email]
Password: Parking
https://parkm.app/permit/community

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you create the account and provide login info?

property_cancel_resident_account.label: Property: Cancel Resident Account
property_cancel_resident_account.intro: Property is requesting cancellation of a resident's permit/account.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Locate the active permit(s)
Set the permit to Delay Cancel (1 week out)
steps.substep: Do NOT cancel immediately. Use Actions > Cancel > Delay Cancellation > set to 1 week. Delete the 'Next Recurring Date' so they are not charged again.

steps.text: If the property/resident also wants a refund: Follow refund process
steps.substep: Forward to accounting@parkm.com with the required info.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you delay cancel (not immediate cancel)?
Did you delete the Next Recurring Date?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_permitting_paid_resident_vehicl.label: Property: Permit Paid Vehicle for Resident
property_permitting_paid_resident_vehicl.intro: Property wants us to purchase a PAID permit on behalf of a resident. Must have a payment method on file and permission to charge.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email
Verify the resident has a payment method on file
steps.substep: Go to Payments & Transactions tab. If no payment method, the resident will need to add one at https://parkm.app/account/login first.

steps.text: Confirm you have permission to charge the payment method
steps.substep: The property or resident must explicitly give permission before you charge.

steps.text: Purchase the permit on the backend
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Select community and permit type > Toggle OFF 'Issue Free Permit' > Select vehicle > Select payment method (credit card) > Toggle OFF 'Do not send email receipt' (so resident gets receipt) > Save.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify payment method on file?
Did you have permission to charge?
Did you send the receipt to the resident?

property_resident_payment_help.label: Property: Resident Payment Help
property_resident_payment_help.intro: Property is helping a resident with a payment issue.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email
Check Payments & Transactions tab for issues
steps.substep: Look for: failed payments, expired cards, missing payment methods, duplicate charges.

steps.text: Identify and resolve the payment issue
steps.substep:
For card updates: Resident needs to update at https://parkm.app/account/login
For billing disputes: Check if charge is legitimate or needs refund
For failed payments: Check card expiry

steps.text: If refund needed: Forward to accounting@parkm.com
Respond to the property with resolution
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you resolve or escalate the payment issue?

property_guest_permits.label: Property: Guest Permits
property_guest_permits.intro: Property is asking about guest permit process, limits, or pricing.

steps.text: Check the property's guest permit setup in parkm.app
steps.substep: Go to the property > check guest permit types, pricing, duration limits, and restrictions.

steps.text: Answer the property's question
steps.substep:
Key info:
- Guest permits are purchased by the RESIDENT for their guest
- Some properties have limits on guest permits
- Guests can pre-purchase and extend permits
- Guest permits vs resident permits are different permit types

steps.text: If the property wants to change guest permit settings: CC the sales rep
steps.substep: CC the property's ParkM sales rep for configuration changes.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you answer the property's guest permit question?

property_potential_leads.label: Property: Potential Lead
property_potential_leads.intro: A new property or complex is interested in ParkM services. Forward to the sales team.

steps.text: Gather the lead information
steps.substep: Note: property name, contact name, email, phone, and what they're interested in.

steps.text: Forward the thread to the sales team
steps.substep: Forward to the appropriate sales channel. Check Zoho CRM for the territory/region.

steps.text: Acknowledge the inquiry to the property
steps.substep: Let them know a sales representative will be in touch shortly.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you forward to the sales team?
Did you acknowledge the inquiry?

sales_rep_asking_for_a_vehicle_to_be_rel.label: Sales Rep: Vehicle Release Request
sales_rep_asking_for_a_vehicle_to_be_rel.intro: Sales rep is asking for a vehicle to be released from a tow/boot. Tow/boot refunds and releases require Internal Manager approval.

steps.text: Search parkm.app for the vehicle in question
Check the vehicle's permit status at the time of the incident
Forward to Internal Managers for review
steps.substep: Let the sales rep know you'll send it to a manager to review. Forward the thread to internalmanagers@parkm.com with a summary.

steps.text: Set ticket to waiting and close when resolved

validation_on_close: Did you forward to Internal Managers?
Did you NOT promise a release?

sales_rep_asking_for_a_vehicle_to_be_gra.label: Sales Rep: Grandfather Request
sales_rep_asking_for_a_vehicle_to_be_gra.intro: Sales rep is requesting a grandfathered (free) permit for a vehicle.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Get vehicle details
steps.substep: Need: license plate, make, model, year, color, state.

steps.text: Issue a free grandfathered permit
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration (1 year default, or as specified). Label 'Grandfathered'. No Next Recurring Date.

steps.text: Confirm with the sales rep
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue the grandfathered permit?
Did you confirm with the sales rep?

towing_or_monitoring_leads.label: Towing/Monitoring Lead
towing_or_monitoring_leads.intro: A towing, booting, ticketing, or monitoring company is reaching out. Forward to Brian McDonough (Enforcement Manager).

steps.text: Determine the nature of the inquiry
steps.substep: Is this a tow company wanting to get set up? A property asking about getting a tow company? Questions about ParkM's towing/monitoring integration?

steps.text: Forward to Brian McDonough
steps.substep: Brian is our Enforcement Manager. He handles setting up tow/boot/ticket/monitoring companies on ParkM. Forward the thread to Brian.

steps.text: If a property is asking about getting a tow company: CC the sales rep
steps.substep: CC the property's sales rep and say: 'I've cc'd your ParkM sales representative, [Name], to assist in selecting a towing, booting, or ticketing company for your community.'

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you forward to Brian McDonough?

the_law_asking_for_information.label: Law Enforcement Request
the_law_asking_for_information.intro: Law enforcement is requesting information. Escalate to Internal Managers immediately.

steps.text: Do NOT respond directly to law enforcement
steps.substep: These requests must be handled by management.

steps.text: Forward immediately to Internal Managers
steps.substep: Forward the entire thread to internalmanagers@parkm.com with 'Law Enforcement Request' in the subject/summary.

steps.text: Set ticket status to Escalated/Waiting

validation_on_close: Did you forward to Internal Managers?
Did you NOT respond directly to law enforcement?
